prompt: Write a single ARO line that logs the message 'Hello, World!' to the console.
Write one ARO statement that retrieves the current time from the clock.
Write a single line that computes the length of the string 'banana'.
Write one ARO statement that converts the string 'hello' to uppercase.
Write one line that hashes the password 'secret'.
Write a single ARO line that adds 7 and 5 and stores the result as <total>.
Write one line that retrieves the git status.
Write a single ARO line that logs the value of <user> to the console.
Write one ARO statement that reads the file 'config.yaml' from the filesystem.
Write a single line that emits a UserCreated event with <user>.
Write a 3-statement ARO snippet that reads a file, uppercases it, and logs the result.
Write a 4-statement snippet that fetches users from a repository, computes the count, and logs it.
Write an ARO snippet that stages all changes, commits with message 'wip', and pushes.
Write a 3-statement snippet that creates a list, computes its length, and logs the length.
Write a snippet that extracts a name from an event, uppercases it, and stores it back in a variable.
Write a 3-statement snippet that computes 3 + 4, multiplies by 2, and logs the result.
Write a snippet that reads two numbers and returns their sum.
Write a snippet that filters a list of users where age > 18 and counts them.
Write a snippet that joins two lists and stores the result as <combined>.
Write a snippet that parses a JSON string and extracts the 'name' field.
Write an ARO user-defined action called DoubleValue that takes a number and returns it doubled.
Write a user-defined action 'Greet' that takes a name and returns a greeting string.
Write a user-defined action 'AddTwoNumbers' that takes two numbers and returns their sum.
Write a user-defined action 'ValidateEmail' that takes an email string and returns OK or Error.
Write a user-defined action 'Square' that takes a number and returns its square.
Write a user-defined action 'FormatDate' that takes a date and a format string.
Write a user-defined action 'CountWords' that takes a string and returns the word count.
Write a user-defined action 'PickRandom' that takes a list and returns a random element.
Write a user-defined action 'Capitalize' that takes a string and uppercases its first letter.
Write a user-defined action 'ComputeTax' that takes a price and a rate and returns the tax.
Write an event handler for the UserCreated event that sends a welcome email.
Write an event handler for OrderPlaced that decrements inventory.
Write an event handler for PaymentFailed that logs the error and notifies the user.
Write an event handler for FileChanged that re-reads the configuration.
Write an event handler for LoginAttempt that records the IP address.
Write an event handler for GitPush that triggers a CI build.
Write an event handler for SubscriptionExpired that downgrades the account.
Write an event handler for ServerStarted that warms up the cache.
Write an event handler for MessageReceived that echoes the message back.
Write an event handler for ImageUploaded that generates a thumbnail.
Write a complete ARO Application-Start that prints Hello and exits.
Write a complete Application-Start that starts an HTTP server on port 8080.
Write a complete Application-Start that watches a directory for changes.
Write a complete Application-Start and Application-End:Success that logs startup and shutdown.
Write a complete Application-Start that opens a TCP echo server on port 9000.
Write a complete Application-Start that starts a WebSocket server and keeps it alive.
Write a complete Application-Start that connects to a database and runs a single query.
Write a complete Application-Start that reads command-line parameters and prints them.
Write a complete Application-Start that loads a configuration file and logs it.
Write a complete Application-Start that prints the current git branch.
Write an ARO feature set for a listUsers operationId that returns all users from a repository.
Write the openapi.yaml plus feature set for a POST /products endpoint.
Write a feature set for getUserById that extracts the id from pathParameters.
Write a feature set for createOrder that validates the request body and emits OrderCreated.
Write a feature set for deleteSession that removes a session from the sessions-repository.
Write a feature set for searchProducts that takes a query parameter and filters results.
Write a feature set for updateProfile that patches a user record.
Write a feature set for healthCheck that returns OK.
Write a feature set for uploadAvatar that accepts a file in the request body.
Write a feature set for login that compares a hash and returns a session token.
Write a repository observer for the users-repository that logs every change.
Write a repository observer for orders-repository that emits OrderUpdated when records change.
Write a feature set that stores a new product into the products-repository.
Write a feature set that retrieves all sessions from the sessions-repository where active = true.
Write an observer that triggers when a user is deleted from the user-repository.
Write a feature set that updates a record in the inventory-repository.
Write a state-guarded handler that triggers only on UserUpdated where role = admin.
Write a feature set that bulk-imports a list of users into the user-repository.
Write a feature set that publishes the count of orders as a global variable.
Write a repository observer that retries on failure.
Refactor this snippet to use the qualifier-as-name syntax: `Compute the <length> from <a>. Compute the <length> from <b>.`
Fix this snippet so it parses: `Log Hello to console.`
Convert this English description to ARO: 'When a user signs up, send them a welcome email and store them in the users repository.'
Convert this Python function to an ARO user-defined action: `def double(x): return x * 2`.
Rewrite this ARO snippet to use lazy evaluation idioms: `Compute the <a> from 1+1. Compute the <b> from <a>+1. Log <b> to the <console>.`
Split this monolithic feature set into one Application-Start and one event handler: `(Foo: My App) { Log "start" to <console>. Emit a <Started: event>. Log "after" to <console>. }`
Add a When guard to this handler so it only runs for admin users: `(OnUserUpdated: UserUpdated Handler) { Log <event> to the <console>. Return an <OK: status> for <log>. }`
Replace direct repository writes with an emitted event in this feature set: `(saveUser: User API) { Store the <user> in the <user-repository>. Return an <OK: status>. }`
Convert this REPL one-liner into a proper feature set: `Log "Hi" to the <console>.`
Refactor a duplicated three-line greeting flow into a single user-defined action called Greet.
What does the Keepalive action do in ARO?
Explain the difference between Application-Start and an event handler.
What is the difference between OWN and EXPORT action roles?
How does ARO's error philosophy work? What is 'code is the error message'?
Explain what the openapi.yaml file does in an ARO application.
What does Publish as <alias> do?
How are events routed to feature sets in ARO?
What is the role of the EventBus?
Explain repository observers and how they differ from event handlers.
What does lazy execution mean in ARO and when is a future forced?
Write an ARO snippet that intentionally fails so the runtime produces a 'Can not ...' message — explain what triggers that message.
Show me an example of using qualifier-as-name with two different `length` results in the same feature set.
Write a feature set with both When guards and a Publish statement.
Write a Compute statement that uses a plugin qualifier called Collections.pick-random on a list.
Write a Git action that clones a repository from a URL into ./local-clone.
Write a feature set that uses a state-guard `where status = pending` on a custom event.
Show a Render action invocation that fills a Mustache-style template.
Write a complete .store file plus a feature set that reads from a seeded repository.
Write an action that uses a list-range slice (e.g., elements 0..3).
Write an event handler that uses typed event extraction with a schema-validated payload.
